channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [6] Interface effects unlock unusual superconductivity in a light-element superconductor
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02605-7

A heterostructure comprising a trilayer of gallium, sandwiched between graphene and silicon carbide, exhibits Ising-type superconductivity driven by quantum confinement and interfacial orbital hybridization. This light-element superconductor retains its superconductivity under in-plane magnetic fields three times larger than the Pauli paramagnetic limit of conventional superconductors.
item.dc:title: Interface effects unlock unusual superconductivity in a light-element superconductor
item.description:
Authors: Authors not available in source RSS

Last/corresponding-author proxy: not available

Score 6/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Ising-type superconductivity and orbital hybridization in a 2D heterostructure; relevant to electronic structure interests.

Tags: #superconductivity #2D-materials #electronic-structure

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02605-7 A heterostructure comprising a trilayer of gallium, sandwiched between graphene and silicon carbide, exhibits Ising-type superconductivity driven by quantum confinement and interfacial orbital hybridization. This light-element superconductor retains its superconductivity under in-plane magnetic fields three times larger than the Pauli paramagnetic limit of conventional superconductors.

item.title: [6] Field-resolved observation of exciton coherence in a van der Waals magnet
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02598-3

Coherent exciton dynamics in antiferromagnetic CrSBr are studied by petahertz field-resolved spectroscopy, revealing how laser-imprinted exciton coherence persists beyond excitation and is modulated by spin and lattice modes.
item.dc:title: Field-resolved observation of exciton coherence in a van der Waals magnet
item.dc:creator: Matthew Yeung, Alexander von Hoegen, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.; last authors: Phillip D. Keathley, Nuh Gedik
Alexander von Hoegen
Emil Viñas Boström
Fangzhou Zhao
Felix Ritzkowsky
Jack B. Maier
Gian Luca Dolso
Christian Heide
Daniel G. Chica
Xavier Roy
Karl K. Berggren
Angel Rubio
Phillip D. Keathley
Nuh Gedik
item.description:
Authors: Matthew Yeung, Alexander von Hoegen, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.; last authors: Phillip D. Keathley, Nuh Gedik

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Phillip D. Keathley, Nuh Gedik

Score 6/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Spectroscopy of a van der Waals magnet (CrSBr) focusing on spin-lattice-exciton coupling.

Tags: #vdW-magnet #spectroscopy #antiferromagnetism

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02598-3 Coherent exciton dynamics in antiferromagnetic CrSBr are studied by petahertz field-resolved spectroscopy, revealing how laser-imprinted exciton coherence persists beyond excitation and is modulated by spin and lattice modes.
